reasoning.typography: Monospace type system (looks like a classic dev font) is distinctive and disciplined, but not groundbreaking — works perfectly for the persona without being generic Inter
reasoning.hierarchy: Clear dominance of name/intro, but post list gets a bit uniform — headlines could be bolder, dates are appropriately subordinate, navigation is clean but not doing heavy visual lifting
reasoning.distinctiveness: Unmistakably personal — 'memes and dreams and software', mechanical keyboards, the circular photo — swap the words and this breaks immediately. Strong individual voice.
reasoning.color: Dark theme with restrained accent usage (underlines on links), minimal decoration, disciplined palette — the magenta/pink underlines provide just enough pop without chaos
reasoning.density: Confident breathing room, not overly padded, allows content to speak — could push density slightly higher in post list but generally well-balanced
reasoning.voice: Structure mirrors the actual person: intro > work > posts > tags. No stencil 'Services' or 'Testimonials' — it's a genuine 'here's who I am and what I make' flow

winning_patterns: Monospace everywhere commits to dev-person aesthetic without apology
Circular photo + keyboard prop immediately signals personality
Inline conversational links ('You should subscribe to my newsletter!') feel human, not corporate
Bio integrates actual interests (mechanical keyboards, memes) not just resume bullets
Tag taxonomy at bottom is functional and humble, not over-designed
Dark theme with subtle accent underlines shows restraint
anti_patterns: Post list could have more typographic variation — titles blend together
Navigation bar is functional but could be bolder/more distinctive
Date positioning (right-aligned italic) is a bit generic blog-template
Some posts lack clear visual hierarchy between title and description
Footer 'View posts by tag' section feels slightly tacked-on visually
